1.texto_original: Percebendo Joab, filho de Seruia, que o coração do rei estava inclinado para Absalão,
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Samuel 14:1

"Percebendo Joab, filho de Seruia, que o coração do rei estava inclinado para Absalão,"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 2 Samuel 14:1

Philological Analysis

"Percebendo Joab, filho de Seruia, que o coração do rei estava inclinado para Absalão,"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Percebendo Joab, filho de Seruia, que o coração do rei estava inclinado para Absalão,"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

7.texto_original: E eis que toda a parentela se levantou contra a tua serva, dizendo: Dá-nos aquele que matou a seu irmão, para que o matemos pela vida de seu irmão, a quem ele matou, de modo que exterminemos também o herdeiro. The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

"Nasceram a Absalão três filhos, e uma filha cujo nome era Tamar; e esta era mulher formosa à vista."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

28.texto_original: Assim ficou Absalão dois anos inteiros em Jerusalém, sem ver a face do rei.
28.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Samuel 14:28

"Assim ficou Absalão dois anos inteiros em Jerusalém, sem ver a face do rei."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

32.texto_original: Respondeu Absalão a Joab: Eis que enviei a ti, dizendo: Vem cá, para que te envie ao rei, a dizer-lhe: Para que vim de Gessur? Melhor me fora estar ainda lá. Agora, pois, veja eu a face do rei; e, se há em mim alguma culpa, que me mate.

33.texto_original: Foi, pois, Joab à presença do rei, e lho disse. Então o rei chamou Absalão, e ele entrou à presença do rei, e se prostrou com o rosto em terra diante do rei; e o rei beijou Absalão.
33.normal:
Devotional Study of 2 Samuel 14:33

"Foi, pois, Joab à presença do rei, e lho disse. Então o rei chamou Absalão, e ele entrou à presença do rei, e se prostrou com o rosto em terra diante do rei; e o rei beijou Absalão."
